reasoning: Q1 is performing strongly at 55ms (63.6% of DuckDB best, 270x faster than PostgreSQL), but is not yet winning (need ≥1.2x faster). This is iteration 3/3 (final budget). High-impact optimizations are identified and concrete: SIMD vectorization of arithmetic (8-12%), L3 cache tuning (5-10%), and zone maps (3-8%). Expected combined gain of 16-30% could reach 39-43ms, approaching or matching DuckDB best. Aggressive final push is warranted.
focus_areas: SIMD vectorization of floating-point arithmetic in lines 175-182 (disc_price, charge calculations) using AVX-512
L3 cache efficiency: reduce morsel_size from 100K to 25K rows to keep working set within 1.4MB L3 per thread
Zone maps for l_shipdate block skipping to reduce I/O latency and page cache pollution
strategy_notes: Apply all three optimizations in combination: SIMD targets the compute-bound arithmetic loop (vectorize disc_price and charge calculations for 8 rows per iteration), morsel reduction targets the memory-bound phase (improve L3 hit rate from ~40% to ~70%), and zone maps target I/O efficiency (skip ~6% of blocks, hide HDD latency). These are complementary and should not interfere. Expected outcome: 39-43ms execution time, which would be competitive with or better than DuckDB best. This is the final iteration — use the full budget to maximize impact.
notes: Risk mitigation: (1) SIMD is well-scoped to lines 175-182, low integration risk. (2) Morsel reduction is conservative (increase sync frequency with proven low contention). (3) Zone maps are read-only and non-invasive. If SIMD fails, fallback to zone maps + prefetching. The query is parallel-scalable, well-structured, and these optimizations follow established patterns in the knowledge base.
